column1: I am in such pain and agony I cannot think.  Help me, good adventurer, please.
column2: 我痛得要死，根本想不了。好心的冒險者，拜託幫幫我。

column1: You don't have to put me out of my misery, you just have to get me out of this trap.
column2: 你不用讓我痛快地死去，只要把我從這個陷阱裡救出來就行。

column1: There seems to be a fox north of the road.
column2: 北邊道路有隻狐狸。

column1: That was strange, no sooner did you kill the fox than it vanished.
column2: 真怪，你剛殺死狐狸，牠就消失了。

column1: Hmmm.  Perhaps it needed a stronger spell.
column2: 嗯…也許得用更強的法術。

column1: Your powerful Open spell springs the trap.
column2: 你強大的開啟術引發了陷阱。

column1: You spring the trap.
column2: 你啟動了陷阱。

column1: In exchange for your kindness, I will give you some advice and a bit of information.  First of all, it sometimes pays off to be polite, even to rude people.
column2: 為了回報你的善心，我會給你一些建議和資訊。首先呢，禮貌待人有時是值得的，就算對方很粗魯也不例外。

column1: As for the amusing tidbit -- Baba Yaga put an enchantment on the Baron's Daughter some years back.  To break the spell you need to talk to the Dryad. Au Revoir, Ta-ta for now, Be seeing you.
column2: 至於這則趣事──芭芭雅嘎多年前在男爵之女身上下了詛咒。要破解詛咒得去和樹精談談。再見，暫時再會，回頭見。

column1: Help me, Brave and Kind Hero.
column2: 幫幫我，勇敢善良的英雄。

column1: Please help me, Brave and Kind Hero.
column2: 拜託幫幫我，勇敢善良的英雄。

column1: Please have mercy on a creature who is in great pain.
column2: 求求你，對這隻深陷痛苦的生物發發慈悲。

column1: The fox does not seem to want to fight.
column2: 狐狸好像不想打架。

column1: The trap seems to be caught on something.  You can't pull it loose.
column2: 陷阱好像卡在什麼東西上。你沒辦法把它拉鬆。

column1: There is an aura of magic around the fox.
column2: 狐狸周圍有股魔法的氣息。

column1: There is no magic present.
column2: 這裡沒有魔法。

column1: There is no point to that.
column2: 這沒意義。

column1: The fox is in such pain that it's already dazzled.
column2: 狐狸痛得已經被眩暈了。

column1: You realize, of course, I really find it hard to relax with this thing on my leg.
column2: 當然，你明白吧，我實在很難在腿上戴著這東西的情況下放鬆。

column1: It looks like a dinosaur trudging grumpily to work.
column2: 看起來像隻恐龍不高興地拖著腳步去工作。

column1: It looks like a dinosaur skipping merrily back home from work.
column2: 看起來像隻恐龍歡快地跳著回家去。

column1: This trap is an especially nasty piece of work.  Besides the inch-long spiky bits, it... well, never mind; suffice it to say that you would not enjoy being caught in it.
column2: 這陷阱特別是個討厭的傢伙。除了那一英吋長的尖刺外，還有…算了；反正你一定不會想被困在裡面。

column1: It appears that a recent avalanche has sealed off the road leading out of the valley.
column2: 看來最近一場雪崩堵住了通往山谷外的道路。

column1: It's a ring formed of large and rather unusual-looking mushrooms.
column2: 那是由又大又奇怪的蘑菇形成的一個圓環。

column1: The fairy ring is luminous at night.
column2: 妖精圓環在夜間會發光。

column1: You pick a handful of the smaller mushrooms and carefully put them away in your backpack.
column2: 你摘下一把小蘑菇，小心翼翼地放進背包裡。

column1: I'll bet you're sorry you said that.
column2: 我打賭你現在後悔說那句話了。

column1: You place the dust carefully away in an empty flask.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把粉末放進空瓶子裡。

column1: You hold out your hand... what else could you do?
column2: 你伸出手…你還能幹什麼呢？

column1: As the fairy dust sifts through your fingers, you realize that you need something in which to hold it.
column2: 當妖精粉末從你的手指間漏下時，你才意識到需要什麼東西來裝著它。

column1: The trees look more vibrant than most of the forest.
column2: 這些樹看起來比森林裡大多數的樹更充滿生氣。

column1: There is a ring of mushrooms in the center of the clearing.
column2: 空地中央有一個蘑菇圓環。

column1: As the fairy dust sifts through your fingers, you realize that you needed something in which to put it.
column2: 當妖精粉末從你的手指間漏下時，你才意識到需要什麼東西來裝著它。

column1: To whom are you talking?
column2: 你在和誰說話？

column1: You detect faint emanations of magic near the ring of mushrooms.
column2: 你在蘑菇圓環附近偵測到微弱的魔法氣息。

column1: It's either a stone, a pebble, a mass of stony material, a solid mineral deposit, a block, or a rock.  I'll let you decide for yourself.
column2: 它是石頭、卵石、一堆石質物質、固態礦物堆積、一塊東西，或一塊岩石。我讓你自己決定。

column1: This used to be a member of a famous rock group, but it decided to split the scene.
column2: 這曾經是個著名搖滾樂團的成員，但它決定離開現場。

column1: The trees seem more dense and lush than the rest of the woods.
column2: 這些樹看起來比森林其他地方更密集、更翠綠。

column1: The trees here look thicker and healthier than in other parts of the forest.
column2: 這裡的樹看起來比森林其他地方更粗壯、更健康。

column1: This man looks very tough.  From his clothing, you guess him to be a member of the Thieves' Guild.
column2: 這個人看起來很硬漢。從他的衣著，你猜他是竊盜公會的成員。

column1: Dead men tell no tales.
column2: 死人不會講話。

column1: He's dead already; don't dull your daggers!
column2: 他已經死了；別磨鈍你的匕首！

column1: You detect no magic here.
column2: 你在這裡沒偵測到魔法。

column1: You waste a spell.
column2: 你浪費了一個法術。

column1: The brigand is too angry to make calm.
column2: 山賊太生氣了，無法被鎮定。

column1: You waste a spell. Fetch is only good for fetching small, visible objects.
column2: 你浪費了一個法術。取物術只能用來取小的、看得見的物體。

column1: Just wait. The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
column2: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

column1: The dead brigand lies very still.
column2: 死山賊靜靜地躺著。

column1: You see a hard-looking character who appears to be a thief.  He must be one of the Brigands!
column2: 你看到一個看起來很硬漢的傢伙，看起來像個盜賊。他一定是其中一個山賊！

column1: Get closer to him.
column2: 靠近他。

column1: The brigand is too angry to calm.
column2: 山賊太生氣了，無法冷靜。

column1: Your Flame Dart streaks towards the startled brigand.
column2: 你的火焰鏢朝驚呆的山賊射去。

column1: You waste a spell.  Fetch is only good for fetching small, visible objects.
column2: 你浪費了一個法術。取物術只能用來取小的、看得見的物體。

column1: Just wait.  The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
column2: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

column1: The buildings of Spielburg can be seen beyond the town wall.
column2: 在城牆外可以看到史畢柏格的建築物。

column1: So what's this about, anyway?
column2: 這到底是怎麼回事？

column1: Her Nibs is starting to get suspicious about us.
column2: 那位高高在上的傢伙開始懷疑我們了。

column1: What's the bee in her bonnet?
column2: 她怎麼了？

column1: Seems the 'hero' wandering around here has her leery.
column2: 看起來在這裡亂晃的『英雄』讓她提防。

column1: She thinks he's going to go for the gold on her head.
column2: 她認為他會去追那頭上的金子（懸賞）。

column1: What's it ta do with us?
column2: 這跟我們有什麼關係？

column1: She's been asking too many questions 'bout us.
column2: 她一直在問太多關於我們的問題。

column1: And the laughing jackass's eyeing me.  I had to sneak out.
column2: 那個愛笑的傻子一直盯著我。我不得不偷偷溜出來。

column1: Then we got to avoid the ambush and use the back for a bit, 'til the heat is off.
column2: 那麼我們得避開伏擊，用後門一陣子，等風吹過去。

column1: You retrieve your dagger.
column2: 你拿回你的匕首。

column1: Hey, watchu doin' here?  Spyin', I'll wager!
column2: 嘿，你在這裡幹嘛？偵查，我敢打賭！

column1: And remember the 'word'.
column2: 還有，記住那個『密碼』。

column1: What word?
column2: 什麼密碼？

column1: The 'word' what lets ya in so that Fred goes away.
column2: 那個『密碼』讓你進去，這樣佛瑞德就會消失。

column1: Oh yeah, sure.  What is it?
column2: 哦，是啊，當然。是什麼？

column1: Hiden Goseke.  Ya better learn it.
column2: 希登·格塞克。你最好學起來。

column1: You think I'm a dummy or somethin'?
column2: 你以為我是個傻瓜或什麼的？

column1: Say the 'word' before ya go in the door or ya might make Fred mad.
column2: 進門前說出那個『密碼』，不然你可能會惹火佛瑞德。

column1: Ya don't want Fred ta get mad.
column2: 你不想讓佛瑞德生氣。

column1: Hey, no problem.  What's the 'word' again?
column2: 嘿，沒問題。那個『密碼』是什麼來著？

column1: Hiden Goseke.
column2: 希登·格塞克。

column1: Hiden Goseke.  Got it.
column2: 希登·格塞克。明白了。

column1: I gotta get back before the Chief misses me.  Be back in a bit.
column2: 我得在頭兒發現我之前回去。等一下就回來。

column1: Hiden Goseke.  See ya.
column2: 希登·格塞克。回見。

column1: Maybe we should just make our move now.
column2: 也許我們該現在就採取行動。

column1: Naw, let's wait for the creep to go first.
column2: 不，我們先等那傢伙離開。

column1: While she's busy with him, we take over.
column2: 趁她忙著應付他的時候，我們接管。

column1: She'll take him out easy, then we take her out.
column2: 她會輕鬆解決他，然後我們解決她。

column1: Where's the back door, then?
column2: 那後門在哪裡？

column1: Where the bouncer hops around.  Ya got your key still?
column2: 保鑣蹦蹦跳跳的地方。你還有鑰匙嗎？

column1: Yeah.
column2: 是的。

column1: Don't lose it.  I got the only other one.
column2: 別丟了。我只有另一把。

column1: You'll haveta search the rock for the keyhole.  It's hidden good.
column2: 你得在石頭上搜尋鑰匙孔。它藏得很好。

column1: The forest is very overgrown near here.
column2: 這附近的森林長得特別茂盛。

column1: You search around this area for a bit, but you don't find anything very useful.
column2: 你在這個區域搜了一會兒，但沒找到什麼有用的東西。

column1: The branches of the brush block the spell from the brigand.
column2: 灌木的枝條阻擋了來自山賊的法術。

column1: The brigand is too angry to calm down.
column2: 山賊太生氣了，沒辦法冷靜下來。

column1: Just wait.  The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
column2: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

column1: You find nothing else on the brigand's body.
column2: 你在山賊的身上找不到別的東西。

column1: You find a single key on the brigand's body, and put it away.
column2: 你在山賊的身上找到一把鑰匙，並把它收起來。

column1: The old archery target looks as though it has not been used in quite some time.
column2: 那個老舊的射箭靶子看起來已經好久沒用過了。

column1: You are using the old archery target for dagger throwing practice.
column2: 你用那個老舊的射箭靶子來練習投擲匕首。

column1: You can feel the holes where daggers and arrows once hit the target.
column2: 你可以感受到匕首和箭曾經擊中靶子的洞孔。

column1: There are too many bushes between you and the target.
column2: 你和靶子之間有太多灌木。

column1: The trees of the forest make this a pleasantly shady archery range.
column2: 森林的樹木使這裡成為一個令人愉快的陰涼射箭場。

column1: This tree is covered with old, dried fruit from last year.
column2: 這棵樹被去年的老舊乾果覆蓋。

column1: You're sharp enough to realize that climbing over these bushes could result in some thorny problems.
column2: 你聰明到意識到爬過這些灌木可能會引起一些棘手的問題。

column1: The fruit on this tree is withered, and swarming with hornets.  You think better about picking it.
column2: 這棵樹上的果子已經枯萎，而且蜜蜂群聚。你決定還是別摘了。

column1: This is the South wall of Spielburg.  An old target leans against the wall.
column2: 這是史畢柏格的南牆。一個老舊的靶子靠在牆上。

column1: There are acorns here and there on the ground.
column2: 地上到處都是橡樹果。

column1: There is a large, slightly glowing acorn on the ground.
column2: 地上有一個很大的、微微發光的橡樹果。

column1: No thanks; the tree already has plenty of acorns.
column2: 不用了；樹上已經有很多橡樹果了。

column1: You needn't dull your blade playing woodsman.
column2: 你不必因為玩木匠就磨鈍你的刀。

column1: As you begin to form thoughts of violence...
column2: 當你開始萌生暴力的念頭時…

column1: Something about the old oak tree makes you reluctant to climb upon it.
column2: 那棵老橡樹有什麼東西讓你不太想爬上去。

column1: The large, gnarled oak seems to draw your attention.
column2: 那棵又大又扭曲的橡樹似乎吸引了你的注意。

column1: You'd better alder your strategy -- the trees mostly just beech about their more poplar neighbors, or pine fir the fjords.  Oak-ay?
column2: 你最好改變你的策略──這些樹大多只是抱怨他們更受歡迎的鄰居，或者為峽灣感到難受。沒問題吧？

column1: The ancient bark crumbles beneath your fingers where thousands of tiny forest denizens have chewed their way towards the heart of this tree.
column2: 古老的樹皮在你的手指下碎裂，數千個微小的森林居民已經咀嚼著通往這棵樹心臟的路。

column1: In decay, this ancient tree lies as a bleak reminder that you really shouldn't have missed that last dentist appointment.
column2: 在衰敗中，這棵古樹躺在那裡，悲涼地提醒你，你真不應該錯過上次的牙醫約診。

column1: The old log is too broken up to talk to you.
column2: 那根老木頭破得太厲害，沒辦法和你說話。

column1: Most of the bushes around here seem to be bramble and are very thorny.
column2: 這裡周圍的大多數灌木似乎是荊棘，非常有刺。

column1: You did WHAT???
column2: 你做了什麼？？？

column1: She's beyond your reach.
column2: 她超出你的範圍。

column1: She's rooted to the ground.
column2: 她根在地上。

column1: The Dryad is not in at the moment.  Please leaf a message with your species name and branch.
column2: 樹精現在不在。請留下你的物種名稱和分支訊息。

column1: The stag remains out of your reach.
column2: 那隻雄鹿仍然超出你的範圍。

column1: Does a Dryad bark?
column2: 樹精會叫嗎？

column1: No, but she's a real birch.
column2: 不會，但她是個真正的難相處傢伙。

column1: Don't make an ash of yourself.
column2: 別讓自己出醜。

column1: The Dryad appears to be a woman made of wood and leaves.
column2: 樹精看起來像是一個由木頭和葉子組成的女人。

column1: The hills are far too far to finger.
column2: 那些山丘太遠，沒辦法碰到。

column1: Majestic mountains rising in the north are silhouetted against the crystal blue sky.
column2: 北邊雄偉的山峰在晶藍色的天空中映出剪影。

column1: You see an acorn fall slowly to the ground.
column2: 你看到一個橡樹果慢慢地掉到地上。

column1: Then you shall aid me, and I shall aid you in your quest.
column2: 那麼你要幫助我，我也會在你的任務中幫助你。

column1: Bring me a seed from the Spore Spitting Spirea of the North that I may plant it elsewhere in order to preserve these rare and magical plants.
column2: 給我帶來一粒來自北邊的孢子噴發繡線菊的種子，這樣我可以在別處種植它，以保護這些稀有的魔法植物。
